title: Stone Prairie Lantern
prompt: Create an original American folk track at 84 BPM in F-sharp minor. Groove: steady brushed strum, soft kick made from foot taps, handclaps, and light shaker; low end: an upright-style acoustic bass that walks gently under open guitar chords. Feature steel-string acoustic guitar, harmonica-like reed tones, mandolin touches, warm piano, and brushed hand percussion, using sparse fills and human timing. Vocal: plainspoken, warm, and close-mic, with a small harmony stack that feels human; no lyrics, only performance direction. Structure: short motif intro, light-percussion verse, passing-bass pre-chorus, emotional harmony chorus, clapless low-tension breakdown, opening-motif fade. Mix with natural dynamics, clear phrasing, smooth reverb tails, and balanced modest-loudness mastering.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, direct folk tune copying, anthem quotes, hymn copying, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, fake crowd chants, muddy guitars

prompt: Create an original Appalachian folk track at 84 BPM in E minor.
